name: Community Legal Services of Mid-Florida, INC.
description_short: Non-profit legal aid organization providing no-cost legal services and education to low-income residents across Central Florida, with 60 years of community service.
description_long:
Community Legal Services of Mid-Florida, Inc. is a long-established non-profit organization celebrating 60 years of providing legal assistance to underserved populations in Central Florida. The organization operates with a mission to enhance the lives of those served through legal support, assistance, and education, positioning legal access as a fundamental right rather than a privilege tied to income.

The organization offers a comprehensive range of free legal services covering family law, financial matters, housing, food assistance navigation, disaster relief, small business and non-profit guidance, fair housing advocacy, elder law, student and children's rights, and domestic violence protection. They conduct regular legal clinics both online and at physical locations (including their Daytona Beach office in Volusia County), scheduling events multiple times per month across different practice areas. Their offerings extend beyond direct legal representation to include legal education and navigation assistance.

What distinguishes Community Legal Services is their emphasis on accessibility through multiple delivery methods—virtual clinics for remote access and in-person clinics at established office locations. Their long operational history (60 years) suggests organizational stability and deep community integration. They serve multiple counties across the region and maintain focus on vulnerable populations including older adults, families in crisis, individuals experiencing housing instability, and those navigating government benefit systems.

As a non-profit legal aid provider, Community Legal Services represents a genuinely free resource with no hidden fees or commercial incentives. The primary limitation is that legal aid organizations typically operate under significant resource constraints, meaning availability may be limited and response times can vary. Eligibility requirements based on income level may apply, though this is not explicitly detailed on their homepage.
pros: Completely free legal services with no cost to low-income clients
60-year operational history demonstrates organizational stability and expertise
Multiple service locations including Daytona Beach office in Volusia County
Flexible access through both online virtual clinics and in-person appointments
Comprehensive practice areas covering family, housing, financial, elder, and consumer law
Regular scheduled legal clinics (multiple events per month across different areas)
Focus on navigation assistance for government benefits like SNAP and disaster relief
Non-profit structure ensures no commercial interests or profit motives
cons: Legal aid organizations typically operate under resource constraints limiting availability
No explicit information about income eligibility requirements or application process on homepage
Limited geographic service area (Mid-Florida region only, not statewide)
Waiting lists or appointment delays possible due to high demand relative to staff capacity
best_for: Low-income families navigating divorce, custody, or family law matters
Individuals facing housing instability or fair housing discrimination
Older adults requiring legal assistance with financial or elder law issues
Small business owners and non-profits seeking affordable legal guidance
diagnosis: Community Legal Services is best for low-income individuals and families in Central Florida who need legitimate legal assistance without financial barriers. The main caveat is that legal aid organizations typically have limited capacity and may have income eligibility requirements, so availability should be confirmed directly through their website or by contacting their offices.
services: Family law legal clinics (divorce, custody, child support)
Fair housing advocacy and discrimination cases
Domestic violence protection and restraining orders
Elder law and services for older adults
Small business and non-profit legal services
General legal education and community workshops
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 122 E Colonial Dr, Suite 200, Orlando, FL 32801
meta_description: Community Legal Services: non-profit providing no-cost legal services to low-income Floridians for 60 years.
